meta_title: For Founders: design with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Solve 'No Portfolio' for SaaS Founders
content.seo_description: Stop letting a lack of portfolio stall your SaaS growth. Learn to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-level, actionable case study.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by using AI-driven design tools to generate high-fidelity, functional prototypes in hours, not weeks. By leveraging Antigravity for UI scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid iterative logic, you can showcase a production-ready MVP that proves your technical competency and validates your market idea instantly.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent killer of ambition. As a founder, the 'no portfolio' stigma creates a paralyzing feedback loop: you cannot secure your first paying customer without proof of work, but you cannot build the proof of work without the time or design pedigree to execute a polished product.

We are moving past the era of manual CSS tweaking and pixel-pushing. By integrating Antigravity for rapid UI generation and Claude Code for intelligent, contextual refactoring, you can architect a stunning, functional SaaS interface that looks like it took a design team of five to build. This is how you reclaim your momentum and ship before the market moves on.

steps.title: Define the Product Architecture
steps.description: Before designing, formalize your MVP scope. Focus on the core value proposition to ensure the AI generates high-conversion components, not just visual fluff.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive UI component map for a SaaS landing page and dashboard, focusing on conversion-driven layout, clean typography, and a modern minimalist aesthetic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --spec 'Draft a technical spec for a SaaS MVP dashboard with user authentication, analytics charts, and a subscription pricing table.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the UI Scaffold
steps.description: Use Antigravity to translate your architectural intent into code. This bypasses the friction of starting from an empty repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React/Tailwind landing page that includes a hero section, feature grid, social proof component, and a clear call-to-action block.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a React project with Tailwind CSS and import the generated components from Antigravity.'

steps.title: Inject Dynamic Logic with Claude
steps.description: Static designs are not enough. Use Claude Code to wire up your components to a mock API, demonstrating true functional capability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a mock API service module for user authentication and data fetching to populate the dashboard components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Connect the dashboard components to the mock API service and ensure the charts render dynamic data props.'

steps.title: Refine the UX and Micro-interactions
steps.description: Add polish through subtle animations and states. This is the difference between a prototype and a premium SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest micro-interaction improvements for the navigation menu and button hover states to improve user engagement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement Framer Motion animations for all page transitions and button interactions to enhance UX.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Host your prototype immediately. A live link is worth a thousand lines of resume text.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration file for Vercel, optimized for a high-performance React application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the build process and generate a deployment summary for Vercel hosting.'

pro_tips: Always prompt for 'semantic HTML' in Antigravity to ensure your AI-generated code is accessible and SEO-friendly from day one.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to maintain consistent design tokens (colors, spacing, font scales) across all generated components.
When building a portfolio, focus on 'edge cases' in your code; showing how you handled an API error is more impressive than a perfect happy-path UI.
Treat your AI-generated code like a junior developer's output; always perform a manual code review to ensure the logic isn't bloated.
Host your project on a custom domain immediately; a live URL like 'app.yourproject.com' builds significantly more trust than a generic Vercel preview link.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, treat it as a living document. Iterate on user feedback daily, document your build process in a blog post to demonstrate 'Build in Public' authority, and begin reaching out to your first 10 prospective users.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to bypass the portfolio hurdle and prove your value to the market. Don't let perfectionism delay your launch. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
